criteria_verification.criterion_text: `write_entity()` redacts every text field via `redact_credentials()` before write
criteria_verification.evidence: write_entity() calls _entity_to_redacted_payload(entity), which serialises via model_dump(mode='json') then recurses through _redact_payload(), applying redact_credentials() to every str leaf (including nested dicts/lists). Verified by TestWriteEntityRedaction (4 tests) — credentials in GateDecision.rationale, OverrideEvent.operator_rationale, CalibrationEvent.question/answer, and arbitrary nested str fields are all replaced with the canonical ***REDACTED-* markers before _dispatch_write is invoked.
criteria_verification.notes: write_entity() calls _entity_to_redacted_payload(entity), which serialises via model_dump(mode='json') then recurses through _redact_payload(), applying redact_credentials() to every str leaf (including nested dicts/lists). Verified by TestWriteEntityRedaction (4 tests) — credentials in GateDecision.rationale, OverrideEvent.operator_rationale, CalibrationEvent.question/answer, and arbitrary nested str fields are all replaced with the canonical ***REDACTED-* markers before _dispatch_write is invoked.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `write_entity()` chooses MCP vs CLI based on availability (mirrors
criteria_verification.evidence: _dispatch_write() implements the 3-tier pattern: Tier 0 calls _write_via_mcp() when importlib.util.find_spec('graphiti_core') is not None; Tier 1 calls _write_via_cli() (asyncio.create_subprocess_exec on 'guardkit graphiti add-context') when shutil.which('guardkit') is on PATH; Tier 2 raises GraphitiUnavailableError. Verified by TestWriteEntityBackendSelection (4 tests covering MCP-preferred, CLI-fallback, neither-available, and CLI non-zero exit wrapped in GraphitiCLIError).
criteria_verification.notes: _dispatch_write() implements the 3-tier pattern: Tier 0 calls _write_via_mcp() when importlib.util.find_spec('graphiti_core') is not None; Tier 1 calls _write_via_cli() (asyncio.create_subprocess_exec on 'guardkit graphiti add-context') when shutil.which('guardkit') is on PATH; Tier 2 raises GraphitiUnavailableError. Verified by TestWriteEntityBackendSelection (4 tests covering MCP-preferred, CLI-fallback, neither-available, and CLI non-zero exit wrapped in GraphitiCLIError).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `fire_and_forget_write()` dispatches the coroutine and returns synchronously
criteria_verification.evidence: fire_and_forget_write() detects the running loop via asyncio.get_running_loop() and uses asyncio.ensure_future(); when no loop is running it spawns a daemon threading.Thread that runs asyncio.run() in the background. Verified by TestFireAndForgetReturnsSync (2 tests) — async-loop variant returns in <20ms while a 50ms slow_write still completes; sync variant returns in <50ms while the background thread completes within 2s.
criteria_verification.notes: fire_and_forget_write() detects the running loop via asyncio.get_running_loop() and uses asyncio.ensure_future(); when no loop is running it spawns a daemon threading.Thread that runs asyncio.run() in the background. Verified by TestFireAndForgetReturnsSync (2 tests) — async-loop variant returns in <20ms while a 50ms slow_write still completes; sync variant returns in <50ms while the background thread completes within 2s.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Failures in fire-and-forget are caught, logged with full entity_id +
criteria_verification.evidence: fire_and_forget_write() wraps the asyncio.ensure_future() call in try/except and attaches a done-callback (_log_future_outcome) that funnels future.exception() into _log_failure(). The thread variant wraps asyncio.run() in try/except. Verified by TestFireAndForgetSwallowsErrors (3 tests) — RuntimeError raised from _dispatch_write does not propagate to the caller from either the async-loop or sync-thread path, and the @negative memory-write-failure-tolerated scenario (both backends unavailable) confirms the pipeline keeps running.
criteria_verification.notes: fire_and_forget_write() wraps the asyncio.ensure_future() call in try/except and attaches a done-callback (_log_future_outcome) that funnels future.exception() into _log_failure(). The thread variant wraps asyncio.run() in try/except. Verified by TestFireAndForgetSwallowsErrors (3 tests) — RuntimeError raised from _dispatch_write does not propagate to the caller from either the async-loop or sync-thread path, and the @negative memory-write-failure-tolerated scenario (both backends unavailable) confirms the pipeline keeps running.

criteria_verification.evidence: _log_failure() emits logger.error('graphiti_fire_and_forget_write_failed', extra={...}) with all five required fields plus exc_info for traceback. Verified by TestStructuredLogShape — the captured LogRecord exposes entity_id/group_id/entity_type/error_class/error_message attributes (logging flattens 'extra' onto the record) and rec.exc_info is not None.
criteria_verification.notes: _log_failure() emits logger.error('graphiti_fire_and_forget_write_failed', extra={...}) with all five required fields plus exc_info for traceback. Verified by TestStructuredLogShape — the captured LogRecord exposes entity_id/group_id/entity_type/error_class/error_message attributes (logging flattens 'extra' onto the record) and rec.exc_info is not None.

criteria_verification.evidence: TestFireAndForgetSwallowsErrors::test_negative_memory_write_failure_tolerated patches both _mcp_backend_available and _cli_backend_available to return False (simulating a complete outage), invokes fire_and_forget_write inside an asyncio loop, and asserts the driver returns True (pipeline keeps running) and a structured failure log is emitted. The test also exercises the GraphitiUnavailableError path through the done-callback.
criteria_verification.notes: TestFireAndForgetSwallowsErrors::test_negative_memory_write_failure_tolerated patches both _mcp_backend_available and _cli_backend_available to return False (simulating a complete outage), invokes fire_and_forget_write inside an asyncio loop, and asserts the driver returns True (pipeline keeps running) and a structured failure log is emitted. The test also exercises the GraphitiUnavailableError path through the done-callback.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Conditionally approved — test collection errors in independent verification. All Player quality gates passed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 351 chars provided.
